title: Mastercard's Jill Kramer says AI can be an engine of human creativity and insight
excerpt: Mastercard's Jill Kramer says marketers need every tool at their disposal, and that AI can spark human creativity
content_html:
When it comes to debates over the role of artificial intelligence in marketing, Jill Kramer, chief marketing and communications officer at Mastercard, is focused on the upside.

"We need to give ourselves permission to embrace the positive first," she said in a video interview for CMO Insider. "Let's see all that it could be."

Kramer joined Mastercard in December 2025 as chief marketing and communications officer, after more than a decade at Accenture.

She said that marketers need to give themselves permission to embrace the positives of AI because it can be an engine to foster creativity and deeper insights. Kramer said it's important to get specific about AI use cases and ask, "Where within the process of strategy and creativity does it fit, and where does it help you raise the bar?"

The power of the Mastercard brand was one of the things that drew Kramer to take on her new position. "It's not something that marketing does," she said. "It's something that the entire company cares about."

The following transcript has been edited for length and clarity.

The brand belongs to everyone at the company

The strength of the brand is a bit mind-blowing, but it comes from something very simple and very true: Mastercard is a company that cares deeply about its brand. It is in the culture. It is not something that marketing does. It's something that the entire company cares about.

One of the reasons that I made the move is because it was an incredible privilege to work for a company where I knew brand was everything to them. You do always wonder when you get inside, when you're behind those doors, "Is that really what it's like from the inside out?" And I'm very, very happy to say that it is. But we all know those brands that are something everyone loves and protects and nurtures and cares for.

So that's one of the best things and one of the great privileges. With that, of course, comes great stress and responsibility. Everybody who has stewarded this brand — all of us, not just the person who sits in the my role, but everyone on the team — is making decisions that take the brand through that next era.

So what I care deeply about is giving ourselves permission to reflect back, to really understand where we are today, and define where we want it to go so it gets there intentionally and strategically and not accidentally.

Marketers need every tool at their disposal today, including AI

It does feel like a lifetime of artificial intelligence already, and it hasn't been that long. I think it was a privilege to be where I was when AI came onto the scene, at Accenture, at a company whose whole focus is: What is the next wave of technology going to do to enterprise functions, industry, et cetera? It gave me a super early peek and permission to learn very, very early on.

I think the biggest thing about AI is that we spend a lot of time debating whether it's the best thing or the worst thing to ever happen to marketing. I wish we would free ourselves of that construct. We, as marketing communications people, know better than anybody that nothing is ever that clear-cut. It absolutely can be both. It very often is both, and it's the decisions that you make that decide which side of the path you're on.

I believe that with the conditions we're facing in the world today, economic pressure, social pressure, global pressures, that we need every tool at our disposal to do the best potential marketing communications that we absolutely can.

AI is absolutely a tool that can and should serve that. If we look at it as what AI does versus what we do, or at the efficiencies and headcount implications only, we're missing the ability to answer the question, "What else could I do that I could never do before because this tool now exists?"

Maybe there's some naiveté built into that, but I think we need to give ourselves permission to embrace the positive first. Let's see all that it could be.

AI can power human creativity and deeper insights

With AI as a tool and an engine of driving creativity, it's really all about the breadth, the aperture that you can open up when you're doing your research, when you're doing your preparation, that will make the ideas flow and the creativity flow.

I was recently in Japan, and we didn't have a ton of time to prepare, but the team there, the strategy, and the cultural deep-dives that we did were mind-blowing and so inspiring. And a lot of that was facilitated by the tools available today to gather, to distill, and to process so much content in a short period of time.

So getting specific on the actual use of AI, and where within the process of strategy and creativity does it fit, and where does it help you raise the bar? The Japan workshop that I recently participated in was a great example of how we probably would have had a similar discussion, but with less breadth and less depth, had AI not been a tool that the team used.

Read next

Erica Iacono is a contributor to Business Insider 

Julia Hood

Julia Hood is editor of CMO Insider, and Executive Editor, Strategic Initiatives and Special Projects at Business Insider. Julia works across the newsroom, collaborating with journalists across verticals to develop editorial series, features, videos, and events. Julia is also the Newsroom AI Lead, working with our editorial team to test and learn applications for artificial intelligence that support our journalism. Here are some of our team's outstanding features:CMO InsiderAI in Action

blocks.html_en: • Artificial Intelligence

sentences.en: Artificial Intelligence
sentences.zh: 人工智能

content_html_zh:
当谈到人工智能在营销中的作用时，万事达卡首席营销和传播官吉尔·克莱默 (Jill Kramer) 重点关注人工智能的好处。

她在 CMO Insider 的视频采访中表示：“我们需要允许自己首先拥抱积极的一面。” “让我们看看它可能是什么。”

克莱默 (Kramer) 在埃森哲 (Accenture) 工作了十多年后，于 2025 年 12 月加入万事达卡 (Mastercard)，担任首席营销和传播官。

她说，营销人员需要允许自己接受人工智能的积极作用，因为它可以成为培养创造力和更深入洞察力的引擎。 克莱默表示，重要的是要具体了解人工智能用例，并询问“它适合战略和创造力过程中的哪些地方，以及它在哪里可以帮助你提高标准？”

万事达卡品牌的力量是吸引克莱默担任新职位的因素之一。 “这不是营销所做的事情，”她说。 “这是整个公司都关心的事情。”

为了长度和清晰度，以下文字记录已被编辑。

品牌属于公司每个人

这个品牌的力量有点令人兴奋，但它来自于一些非常简单和真实的事情：万事达卡是一家非常关心自己品牌的公司。 这是在文化中。 这不是营销所做的事情。 这是整个公司都关心的事情。

我做出这一举动的原因之一是因为在一家我知道品牌对他们来说就是一切的公司工作是一种令人难以置信的荣幸。 当你进入里面，当你在那些门后面时，你确实总是想知道，“从内到外真的是这样吗？” 我非常非常高兴地说确实如此。 但我们都知道那些每个人都喜爱、保护、培育和关心的品牌。

所以这是最好的事情之一，也是最大的特权之一。 当然，随之而来的是巨大的压力和责任。 每个管理这个品牌的人——我们所有人，不仅仅是担任我角色的人，而是团队中的每个人——都在做出带领品牌度过下一个时代的决策。

因此，我非常关心的是允许我们自己进行反思，真正了解我们今天所处的位置，并定义我们希望它去向的地方，以便它有意识地、战略性地而非偶然地到达那里。

营销人员如今需要一切可以使用的工具，包括人工智能

确实感觉人工智能已经度过了一生，而且时间并没有那么长。 当人工智能出现时，我很荣幸能在埃森哲公司，这家公司的全部重点是：下一波技术将对企业职能、行业等产生什么影响？ 它让我能够尽早了解并允许我非常非常早地学习。

我认为人工智能最重要的一点是，我们花了很多时间争论它对于营销来说是最好的事情还是最坏的事情。 我希望我们能够摆脱这种观念。 作为营销传播人员，我们比任何人都更清楚，没有什么是那么明确的。 绝对可以两者兼而有之。 通常两者都是，而你所做的决定决定了你站在道路的哪一边。

我相信，在当今世界面临的经济压力、社会压力、全球压力等条件下，我们需要利用一切可用的工具来尽我们所能进行最佳的潜在营销传播。

人工智能绝对是一个可以而且应该服务于此的工具。 如果我们将其视为人工智能所做的事情与我们所做的事情，或者仅着眼于效率和人员数量的影响，我们就无法回答这个问题：“因为现在有了这个工具，我还能做哪些我以前永远做不到的事情？”

也许其中有一些天真的成分，但我认为我们需要允许自己首先拥抱积极的一面。 让我们看看它可能是什么。

人工智能可以增强人类创造力和更深入的洞察力

人工智能作为一种工具和驱动创造力的引擎，真正重要的是广度，当你做研究、做准备时你可以打开的光圈，这将使想法流动和创造力流动。

我最近在日本，我们没有太多的时间来准备，但那里的团队、战略以及我们所做的文化深入研究令人兴奋且鼓舞人心。 其中很大一部分是由当今可用的工具促进的，这些工具可以在短时间内收集、提炼和​​处理如此多的内容。

那么，具体了解人工智能的实际使用，它在战略和创造力过程中适合什么地方，以及它在哪里可以帮助你提高标准？ 我最近参加的日本研讨会就是一个很好的例子，说明如果人工智能不是团队使用的工具，我们可能会进行类似的讨论，但广度和深度都会减少。

阅读下一篇

艾丽卡·艾科诺 (Erica Iacono) 是《商业内幕》的撰稿人

Julia Hood 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

Julia Hood 是《CMO Insider》的编辑，也是《Business Insider》战略计划和特别项目的执行编辑。 朱莉娅在新闻编辑室工作，与各个行业的记者合作开发社论系列、专题、视频和活动。 Julia 也是 Newsroom AI 主管，与我们的编辑团队合作测试和学习支持我们新闻业的人工智能应用程序。 以下是我们团队的一些突出特点：CMO InsiderAI in Action
